eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent while Myntra and Meesho show strong but price-distorted demand; no clean signal.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong presence
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present at full price.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong presence but at 65% discount with low rating; demand is discount-driven.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand genuine style preference or purely price-driven?
conflicts.proves: The style sells when heavily discounted or at very low price.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has standalone appeal at higher price points.

convergences.title: Volume at low price points
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (discounted) and Meesho (low price) show high unit sales.
convergences.proves: There is demand for this style when price is the primary driver.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand is sustainable or style-driven.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: The style shows strong volume on Myntra and Meesho, indicating broad appeal at low price points. If sourced cheaply, it could move units in value-fashion channels.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: All demand signals are price-distorted: Myntra's sales are driven by a 65% liquidation discount, and Meesho's volume is at ₹299. No premium or organic demand exists. Internal POS has no history, and no social or ad data to validate trend authenticity.

nykaa.reason: No products found; premium demand absent.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume but low price and moderate rating; reseller growth not accelerating.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: All signals are price-driven with no clean demand evidence. Without internal POS, social buzz, or ad data, capital commitment is premature.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Would this style sell at a moderate price (₹500-800) without heavy discounting?

eval-premium-mirage.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand strong on Nykaa but absent on Meesho; Myntra shows moderate clean signal

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho complete absence
conflicts.nykaa_says: 3 full-price products at ₹1850, high ratings, strong demand
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate presence at 15% discount, clean signal
conflicts.meesho_says: No products, zero units sold, no resellers
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't work for value-fashion, or is Meesho simply not the right channel?
conflicts.proves: Premium demand exists at full price on Nykaa
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether demand exists at lower price points or in mass market

convergences.title: Nykaa and Myntra both show positive demand at premium price points
convergences.detail: Nykaa has 3 full-price products at ₹1850 with 4.6★; Myntra has 1 product at 15% discount with 4.1★. Both indicate style-driven demand with low discount distortion.
convergences.proves: The style has appeal in premium segments
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this appeal translates to broader market or is sustainable

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong full-price demand for this premium kurti style, indicating genuine premium consumer interest. Myntra's moderate presence with low discount further supports style-driven demand. If the trend expands to mass market, there could be significant upside.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence is a major red flag for value-fashion viability. The trend may be confined to premium buyers only. Without internal POS data or competitor ad/social buzz, the evidence is thin and concentrated in premium channels.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence with full-price products, high ratings, and substantial reviews

myntra.reason: Moderate presence with clean discount context (Strategic Value Pricing), good ratings

meesho.reason: Complete absence of data; no products or resellers

internal.reason: No prior data available; cannot confirm or contradict

eval-premium-mirage.bet_rationale: Premium demand is validated by Nykaa and Myntra, but Meesho absence and lack of internal/social/ads data create uncertainty. A small trial in premium-focused stores can test real demand without overcommitting.
eval-premium-mirage.key_uncertainty: Will this style gain traction in mass market (Meesho) or remain a premium niche?

eval-mass-only.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Strong Meesho demand conflicts with absent Nykaa and discount-driven Myntra presence.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Meesho strong
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand for this kurti.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at low price point.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this trend viable only in value-fashion channels, or can it be positioned at higher price points?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists but is concentrated in price-sensitive segments.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that demand will translate to premium or full-price channels.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount dependency vs Meesho volume
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate rank at 35% discount, unclear driver.
conflicts.meesho_says: High volume at low price, no discount needed.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Myntra demand driven by style or by the discount?
conflicts.proves: Demand may be price-driven rather than style-driven.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that style preference is the primary driver.

convergences.title: Meesho and Myntra both show demand
convergences.detail: Both sources indicate consumer interest, though at different price points and discount levels.
convergences.proves: There is some level of demand for this kurti.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that demand is consistent across channels or sustainable at higher prices.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume with accelerating reseller growth, indicating a potential mass-market trend. The low price point (₹199) and high unit sales suggest broad appeal in tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa absence and Myntra's discount dependency raise concerns about premium viability. Demand may be purely price-driven, and the trend may not sustain at higher price points or in full-price channels.

nykaa.reason: No data available; absence is a strong signal of no premium demand.

myntra.reason: Moderate presence but discount context unclear; review velocity down-weighted 25%.

meesho.reason: Strong presence with high volume and accelerating reseller growth; clean signal.

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot validate or contradict.

eval-mass-only.bet_rationale: Meesho signal is strong but conflicts with absent Nykaa and discount-driven Myntra. A small trial in value-fashion channels can validate real demand without overcommitting capital.
eval-mass-only.key_uncertainty: Will demand sustain at a slightly higher price point (e.g., ₹399) without deep discounting?

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium, mass, and value segments with no discount distortion

convergences.title: Cross-segment demand convergence
convergences.detail: Nykaa (premium, full-price), Myntra (mass, low discount), and Meesho (value, high volume) all show strong signals with clean pricing. No source contradicts another.
convergences.proves: The style has broad appeal across price-sensitive and premium buyers, indicating genuine trend strength.
convergences.cannot_prove: Long-term sustainability; local store performance; margin impact at different price points.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across all three external sources with no discount distortion. Nykaa confirms premium appeal at full price, Myntra shows mass-market traction with strategic pricing, and Meesho indicates explosive volume in value segment with accelerating reseller growth. This suggests a broad-based trend with potential for high sell-through.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data exists to validate local demand. Meta Ads and YouTube data are absent, so competitor conviction and organic buzz are unknown. The Meesho volume at ₹349 may be price-driven, but the low discount on Myntra and full-price Nykaa presence mitigate this risk.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence with full-price products and high ratings; clean signal.

myntra.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high review velocity, no noise flags.

meesho.reason: High volume, accelerating reseller growth, good rating; clean signal.

internal.reason: No data available; cannot confirm or contradict.

eval-golden-convergence.bet_rationale: All three external sources converge with strong, clean demand signals across price tiers. The absence of internal data is a gap but not a contradiction, and the pattern warrants a wider commitment to multiple stores.
eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: Will local store demand mirror the strong external signals, given no prior internal data?

YouTube haul videos for Golden Kurti increase significantly
eval-golden-convergence.demand_integrity: CLEAN — all products are Strategic Value Pricing or full-price; no discount distortion detected.

convergences.detail: Both Nykaa (0% discount) and Myntra (20% discount, Strategic Value Pricing) show no distress pricing, indicating genuine demand rather than discount-driven sales.

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong premium demand at full price with high ratings, and Myntra confirms moderate demand with clean pricing. This suggests a genuine niche appeal that could work in premium or curated segments.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence indicates no mass-market traction, and lack of social buzz or competitor ad data leaves demand unvalidated beyond two sources. Internal POS has no history, so risk of overestimating demand is high.

chanderi-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa/Myntra show strong demand, but Internal POS says similar SKU underperformed

conflicts.title: Internal POS contradicts external demand signals
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at full price
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong organic demand at low discount
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak imitation demand at low price
conflicts.internal_says: Similar Chanderi silk kurti underperformed (38% sell-through, 18% returns)
conflicts.buyer_question: Will a straight cut at ₹699-899 overcome the durability and wash issues seen in our previous Chanderi SKU?
conflicts.proves: External demand exists for Chanderi straight kurti at premium and mass premium
conflicts.cannot_prove: That our specific customer base will adopt it given past performance

convergences.title: Nykaa, Myntra, and Meta Ads converge on Chanderi straight kurti demand
convergences.detail: Nykaa shows full-price premium demand, Myntra shows strong organic demand at low discount, and two competitors run long-running ads for similar product at ₹899
convergences.proves: The style has validated demand across premium and mass premium channels
convergences.cannot_prove: That the demand is durable or that our store base will replicate it

chanderi-straight.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra show strong, clean demand for Chanderi straight kurti with zari border at ₹899-1,450. Competitors are advertising it with conviction. The trend has trickle-down potential from premium to value-fashion.
chanderi-straight.catch_summary: Internal POS data from a similar Chanderi silk kurti shows underperformance: 38% sell-through, 18% returns, metro-only viability. Our core customers found the fabric too delicate. Meesho shows only weak imitation demand at lower price.

nykaa.reason: Full-price products, strong ratings, editorial placement

myntra.reason: High rank, low discount, high review velocity

meesho.reason: Imitation product, low rating, moderate volume

internal.reason: Direct sales data from similar SKU, clear underperformance

chanderi-straight.bet_rationale: External signals are strong but internal POS contradicts with clear underperformance of a similar SKU. A small trial in metro stores can validate whether the straight cut and lower price point change the outcome.
chanderi-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a ₹699-899 straight cut Chanderi kurti with zari border overcome the durability and wash complaints seen in our previous Chanderi SKU?

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 competitors advertising. Biba 14 days (emerging), Aurelia 21 days (strong). Both cotton, A-line/straight.
reasoning_trace.proves: Competitors have conviction in block-print cotton kurtis. Aurelia's 21-day ad suggests sustained demand.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether ad-driven demand is incremental or cannibalizing organic sales.
reasoning_trace.tension: Ad activity supports demand but YouTube buzz is low — suggests paid push, not organic trend.

reasoning_trace.signal: 10 haul videos from 9 creators. Affiliate link density 0.1/video. Low commercial interest.
reasoning_trace.proves: Some organic creator interest exists, but not driven by affiliate incentives.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether consumer demand is strong. Low video count may reflect category maturity, not lack of interest.
reasoning_trace.tension: Low buzz contrasts with strong sales signals from Myntra, Meesho, and Internal POS. Suggests demand is functional, not aspirational.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Myntra/Ajio discount distortion vs Meesho low-price volume vs Internal POS proven success. YouTube low buzz vs Meta ad conviction.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources except Nykaa (expected absence) show positive demand. Internal POS confirms pattern. Meesho and Myntra both show strong volume.

blockprint-cotton.headline: All sources converge on strong demand for block-print cotton kurti at value-fashion price points. Internal POS confirms proven winner.

convergences.title: Strong multi-channel demand
convergences.detail: Myntra/Ajio (strong presence), Meesho (15k+ units), and Internal POS (78% sell-through) all show robust demand for block-print cotton kurtis.
convergences.proves: The style has broad appeal across organized retail, value-fashion, and our own stores.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the current batch of designs will match past performance (design fatigue).

convergences.title: Internal POS validates external signals
convergences.detail: Our own store data shows 78% sell-through, 44% margin, 4% returns, and 34% repeat buy rate for a similar past SKU. This aligns with strong external signals.
convergences.proves: The category is a core strength for our business with proven profitability.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether we should increase order quantity beyond last year's level.

convergences.title: Competitor ad activity confirms conviction
convergences.detail: Aurelia (21 days) and Biba (14 days) are actively advertising block-print cotton kurtis, indicating sustained commercial interest.
convergences.proves: Competitors see this as a viable category worth ad spend.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the ads are driving incremental demand or just capturing existing demand.

blockprint-cotton.upside_summary: Block-print cotton kurti is a proven winner across multiple channels. Internal POS shows 78% sell-through and 44% margin with low returns. Meesho's 15k+ units and Myntra/Ajio's strong presence indicate broad demand. Competitor ad activity confirms commercial conviction. Under-ordering last year suggests upside from increasing buy quantity.
blockprint-cotton.catch_summary: Myntra/Ajio discount distortion on 2 of 3 products raises caution about price-driven demand. YouTube social buzz is low, suggesting the trend is functional rather than aspirational. Design fatigue is a risk given past success. Meesho reseller growth is not accelerating, which may indicate plateauing demand in tier-2/3/4 markets.

nykaa.reason: Absence is expected for value-fashion category. Not a negative signal.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but 2/3 products have moderate discount with unclear driver. Discount distortion risk high.

meesho.reason: High volume, strong reseller count, clean data. No noise flags.

internal.reason: Direct sales data with high sell-through, margin, and repeat rate. Most reliable source.

blockprint-cotton.bet_rationale: Multiple clean signals converge: Internal POS confirms proven success, Meesho shows strong volume, Myntra/Ajio has strong presence, and competitors advertise. The only caution is moderate discount on some Myntra products, but overall demand integrity is high. Increase order quantity across stores.
blockprint-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will current block-print designs perform as well as the previous Jaipur Block Print Cotton Kurti (AW23) or is there design fatigue?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth accelerates above 18% MoM
Myntra/Ajio discount levels drop below 20% for Aurelia products
YouTube haul video count exceeds 20/month with affiliate density >0.5
Internal POS sell-through for new batch drops below 70% in first 30 days
missing_evidence: Nykaa data is absent but expected — no conflict
No live marketplace data from Amazon/Google Shopping
No data on specific design variations within block-print category
blockprint-cotton.demand_integrity: High — internal POS and Meesho show clean signals. Myntra/Ajio has moderate discount noise but overall positive.
noise_flags_present: Moderate Discount — Unclear Driver (2 products on Myntra/Ajio)

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹1,490, 25% off, 4.1★, 95 reviews, editorial placement
reasoning_trace.proves: Premium D2C brands are testing Bandhani prints at higher price points
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand exists at ₹1,490+ or if this is a niche offering
reasoning_trace.tension: Weak signal; no full-price products; contrasts with Meesho's volume at ₹279

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹649, 20% off, rank 35, 4.3★, 22 reviews/month, Strategic Value Pricing
reasoning_trace.proves: Clean demand signal at mass-retail price with low discount distortion
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether rank 35 is sustainable or driven by limited competition
reasoning_trace.tension: Moderate presence aligns with Meesho's emerging volume but Nykaa is weak

reasoning_trace.signal: 4,200+ units sold, 85 resellers, +35% MoM growth, ₹279, 4.1★, 980 reviews
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong price-sensitive demand in tier-2/3/4, especially Gujarat/Rajasthan/Maharashtra
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this demand is style-driven or purely price-driven at ₹279
reasoning_trace.tension: Volume is high but price is far below trend average; may be price-driven

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar SKU sold 52% in 95 stores, regional success in West India, failure in South/East
reasoning_trace.proves: Bandhani is a regional bet, not national; strong in Gujarat/Rajasthan
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a different design could unlock national appeal
reasoning_trace.tension: Confirms Meesho's regional pattern but contradicts any national rollout assumption

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 competitor (Libas) advertising Bandhani straight kurti for 7 days at ₹649
reasoning_trace.proves: One competitor is testing the style with moderate conviction
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this is a sustained trend or a short test
reasoning_trace.tension: Low ad density suggests trend is not yet heavily pushed by competitors

reasoning_trace.signal: 10 haul videos, 10 unique creators, 0.1 affiliate links/video
reasoning_trace.proves: Organic creator interest exists but is not commercially driven
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this organic interest translates to purchase intent
reasoning_trace.tension: Low affiliate density suggests genuine but small-scale interest

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Meesho shows strong volume at ₹279 but Nykaa and Myntra show limited premium/mid-market presence. Internal POS confirms regional nature, conflicting with any national strategy.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources point to Bandhani print having demand in Western India. Myntra and Meesho both show clean signals (low discount, good ratings).

bandhani-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho volume at ₹279 conflicts with weak premium signals; internal POS confirms regional limitation

conflicts.title: Price-driven vs style-driven demand
conflicts.nykaa_says: Limited premium presence at ₹1,490 with discount
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at ₹649 with low discount (Strategic Value Pricing)
conflicts.internal_says: Similar SKU sold 52% at ₹599-799, regional success only
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Meesho volume driven by the low price point or genuine style preference?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani has demand at multiple price points, but the volume is concentrated at the lowest price
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend can sustain at ₹499-799 without deep discounting

conflicts.title: Regional vs national demand
conflicts.nykaa_says: No regional data, but product is available nationally online
conflicts.myntra_says: No regional data, but product is available nationally online
conflicts.meesho_says: Demand concentrated in Gujarat, Rajasthan, Maharashtra
conflicts.internal_says: Strong in Ahmedabad, Surat, Jaipur; weak in Chennai, Bangalore, Kolkata
conflicts.buyer_question: Should this be a regional buy for Western India only?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani print demand is geographically concentrated in Western India
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether a different colorway or marketing could unlock other regions

convergences.title: Bandhani print has genuine demand in Western India
convergences.detail: Meesho shows 4,200+ units in Gujarat/Rajasthan/Maharashtra; internal POS confirms strong sell-through in Ahmedabad, Surat, Jaipur.
convergences.proves: The style resonates with consumers in Western India at multiple price points
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this demand extends to other regions or price bands

convergences.title: Clean demand signals at mid-price point
convergences.detail: Myntra product at ₹649 has Strategic Value Pricing (20% off, 4.3★, 22 reviews/month) indicating genuine style preference, not discount-driven.
convergences.proves: At ₹649, the style can sell without heavy discounting
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this is scalable beyond one SKU

bandhani-straight.upside_summary: Bandhani print straight kurti shows strong regional demand in Western India with clean signals at mid-price (₹649) and high volume at value price (₹279). Internal POS confirms similar pattern. Competitor ad testing and organic YouTube buzz suggest growing interest.
bandhani-straight.catch_summary: Demand is heavily regional — national rollout risks poor performance in South and East India. Meesho volume may be price-driven rather than style-driven. Premium presence is weak, and only one competitor is actively advertising.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found, discounted, moderate rating — limited evidence

myntra.reason: Clean signal with Strategic Value Pricing, good rating, moderate review velocity

meesho.reason: High volume, accelerating reseller growth, good rating — strong ground-level signal

internal.reason: Direct sales data with clear regional pattern — high relevance

bandhani-straight.bet_rationale: Regional demand is confirmed but national potential is unproven. A small batch focused on Western India stores can validate the trend at ₹499-799 without overcommitting.
bandhani-straight.key_uncertainty: Can a ₹599-799 Bandhani straight kurti achieve >65% sell-through in stores outside Gujarat/Rajasthan?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth exceeds +50% MoM
Myntra rank enters top 20 with sustained review velocity
Competitor Meta ads increase to 3+ brands running 21+ days
Internal POS sell-through in a non-Western region test store exceeds 50%
missing_evidence: Nykaa full-price product data for Bandhani straight kurti
YouTube haul videos with high affiliate density (>0.5 links/video)
Competitor ad data from more than one brand
bandhani-straight.demand_integrity: Mixed — Meesho volume is strong but price-sensitive; Myntra signal is clean; internal data shows regional limitation

reasoning_trace.signal: No products found; presence absent.
reasoning_trace.proves: No premium D2C demand for this trend at ₹800+ price point.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand exists at lower price points or in other channels.
reasoning_trace.tension: Absent while Myntra, Meesho, and Internal POS show strong demand — suggests trend is value-driven, not premium.

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products found, both Genuine Volume Driver but Paid Visibility; avg rank 3, avg rating 4.05, 185 reviews/mo.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong marketplace demand at ₹799-849 with moderate discounts (35-40%) accelerating real trend.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand would persist without discount or ad spend; rank may be inflated by paid visibility.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong signal but discount and paid visibility introduce noise; conflicts with Nykaa absence but aligns with Meesho and Internal POS.

reasoning_trace.signal: 28,000+ units sold, 520 resellers, 3.6★ rating, price ₹399, reseller growth +12%.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong volume demand in tier-2/3/4 markets at value price; reseller network confirms ground-level pull.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or purely price-driven; rating is mediocre.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong volume aligns with Myntra and Internal POS; price gap (₹399 vs ₹799) suggests price sensitivity but not conflict — different segments.

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar past SKU sold 82% sell-through in 200 stores, 38% margin, 41% repeat rate, 6% returns, stockouts in 68 stores.
reasoning_trace.proves: Proven top-seller for our base; set format (kurti+palazzo) is the value proposition; demand not saturated internally.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether next season's designs will maintain velocity; whether external saturation will eventually affect our sales.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong internal success contradicts any notion of over-saturation; aligns with Myntra and Meesho external demand.

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 competitors advertising: Libas (35 days, strong) and Westside (18 days, emerging).
reasoning_trace.proves: Competitors have conviction in this trend; Libas has long-running ad indicating sustained demand.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether ad spend is driving demand or capturing existing demand; Westside ad is still emerging.
reasoning_trace.tension: Moderate ad conviction supports demand but not overwhelming; aligns with Myntra and Meesho signals.

reasoning_trace.signal: 10 haul videos from 9 creators, low affiliate density (0.3 links/video).
reasoning_trace.proves: Some organic creator interest, not heavily commercialized.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether buzz is growing or plateauing; low density may indicate early stage or niche appeal.
reasoning_trace.tension: Low buzz contrasts with strong marketplace and internal sales; suggests demand is functional/outfit-driven rather than trend-driven.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho/Internal strong — trend is value-driven, not premium. Myntra discount and paid visibility introduce noise but discount context is Genuine Volume Driver. YouTube buzz low despite strong sales — demand is practical, not hype-driven.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Myntra, Meesho, and Internal POS all show strong demand across price tiers and regions. Internal POS confirms the set format is a proven winner. Competitor ads add conviction.

fusion-palazzo.headline: All sources converge on strong demand for fusion kurti+palazzo sets across value and mass segments, with internal POS confirming proven success.

convergences.title: Strong multi-channel demand
convergences.detail: Myntra (strong with Genuine Volume Driver), Meesho (28k+ units), and Internal POS (82% sell-through) all show robust demand across price points and regions.
convergences.proves: The trend has broad appeal from tier-1 to tier-4 markets.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable without discounts or ad support.

convergences.title: Internal POS validates external signals
convergences.detail: Internal POS shows 82% sell-through, 41% repeat rate, and stockouts in 68 stores, confirming that external demand translates to real sales for our base.
convergences.proves: Our customer sees the set as a complete outfit value proposition, not just a kurti.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether next season's designs will maintain velocity.

convergences.title: Competitor ad conviction supports trend
convergences.detail: Libas running 35-day ad and Westside 18-day ad indicate competitors see sustained demand.
convergences.proves: The trend is not a flash in the pan; competitors are investing.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether ad spend is driving incremental demand or just capturing share.

fusion-palazzo.upside_summary: Strong multi-channel demand from Myntra (Genuine Volume Driver), Meesho (28k+ units), and Internal POS (82% sell-through) confirms broad appeal. The set format (kurti+palazzo) is a proven value proposition with high repeat rate (41%) and low returns (6%). Competitor ads add conviction.
fusion-palazzo.catch_summary: Nykaa absence indicates no premium demand at ₹800+; trend is value-driven. Myntra products have Paid Visibility flags (rank weight halved) and discounts (35-40%), though discount context is Genuine Volume Driver. YouTube buzz is low, suggesting demand is practical rather than hype-driven.

nykaa.reason: No products found; absent signal provides no data.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but Paid Visibility flags reduce rank reliability; discount context is Genuine Volume Driver.

meesho.reason: High volume (28k+ units), strong reseller count (520), and growing (+12% MoM) — clean ground-level signal.

internal.reason: Proven success with 82% sell-through, 38% margin, and stockouts — highest quality evidence.

fusion-palazzo.bet_rationale: All three external channels (Myntra, Meesho) and Internal POS converge on strong demand. The set format is a proven winner with high repeat rate. Competitor ads add conviction. Expand to more stores and regions.
fusion-palazzo.key_uncertainty: Will the next season's designs maintain the same velocity, or will design fatigue set in?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth slows or turns negative
Myntra discount deepens beyond 50%
Internal POS sell-through drops below 70%
New competitor ads stop or decrease
missing_evidence: Nykaa data (premium channel) — absent, but not needed for value-fashion
YouTube social buzz depth — low, but demand is practical not hype-driven
Long-term repeat purchase data beyond one season
fusion-palazzo.demand_integrity: Genuine Volume Driver across Myntra and Meesho; internal POS confirms real demand. Discounts are accelerating a real trend, not substituting for demand.
